title: TAP media preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"adgqj","text":"This protocol describes the preparation of TAP media. Usually used for growing algae cells, as Chlamydomonas reinhardtii. The protocol is derived from the protocol descrived at https://www.chlamycollection.org/methods/media-recipes/tap-and-tris-minimal/. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":177,"length":76}],"data":[]},{"key":"9u6ir","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3vels","text":"[Gorman, D.S., and R.P. Levine (1965) Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 54, 1665-1669]","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"BB4C73D7C42811EA9E0E0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.chlamycollection.org/methods/media-recipes/tap-and-tris-minimal"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"eke2l","text":"All steps described in this protocol are intended to be conducted in a research laboratory.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"f6mst","text":"Prepare stock solutions","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6v44m","text":"Separate flasks to distribute the prepared media for autoclavation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cgsqv","text":"Separate magnetic stirrer for mixing","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fc2sk","text":"Large enough becker for media preparation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a0eqf","text":"Pippetes/volume measuring apparatus for components","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eds8n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5nr3b","text":"Use EPIs at all times. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dn15n","text":"Concentrated acetic acid solution used during preparation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add approximatelly 90 % volume of ddH 2 0 to a large enough vessel. Add a magnetic bar and start and keep mixing with a magnetic stirrer during the preparation. Place pH probe electrode in the solution for pH monitoring For 1 L, final media volume Add 10 mL, 2M Tris base (e.g. Trizma) Add 10 mL, Solution A Add 1 mL, Phosphate solution Add 1 mL, Hutner`s trace solution Add 1 mL, Glacial acetic acid , then add drops until pH 7 is reached Stop mixing, and add dd H 2 0 until 1 L, final volume is reached Start mixing again until complete mixing is achieved. (5 minutes should suffice). Componets concentration and informations. %s %s Hutner`s trace composition and protocol for preparation can be found here .

Transfer the newlly prepared media to flasks for autoclavation Typically: 1L flasks with blue caps Erlenmeyers with a max volume capacity of 100 mL are filled with 50 mL, capped with alluminium foil (2 layers) Erlenmeyers with a max volume capacity of 500 mL are filled with 250 mL, capped with alluminium foil (2 layers)

Place flasks inside the autoclave (For flasks with lid, make sure it is loosen enough to allow vapor passage) Set autoclavation to 121 °C for at least 00:15:00 , 15 psi. After autoclavation, wait media to cool down and it is ready for use.
creator: Joao Vitor Molino
